candidate_summary: Per-sample aging-timestamped loss-weight exponential decay with per-category half-life T_patina + 5% cracked-bowl intentional-noise reservoir for epistemic humility + curvature-tuned schedule for SFT/RL continual learning.

results.name: MSSR Memory-Aware Adaptive Replay Continual LLM Fine-Tuning
results.description: Ebbinghaus forgetting curve memory retention time-dependent decay; inverse-retention weighting + adaptive replay intervals.
results.rationale: MSSR uses time-dependent decay + inverse-retention weighted replay which is functionally near-identical to per-sample timestamped patina decay loss-weight attenuation. Same artefact (per-sample weighted decay replay) + same effect (controlled forgetting in CL).

results.name: FOREVER Forgetting Curve-Inspired Memory Replay LLM
results.description: Model-centric virtual Ebbinghaus days calibrated by accumulated parameter-update magnitude; replay triggered by model learning progress.
results.rationale: FOREVER virtual-Ebbinghaus days = effectively per-sample timestamped aging schedule for replay. Same artefact (forgetting-curve weighted replay) + same effect (graceful decay of sample influence over training time).

results.name: SuRe Surprise-Driven Prioritised Replay Continual LLM
results.description: Dual-learner fast+slow LoRA + EMA merge; surprise-prioritized replay for CL.
results.rationale: Surprise-prioritized replay + EMA merge addresses CL via a different mechanism (priority by surprise + parameter EMA, not per-sample timestamped exponential decay). Some functional overlap (replay buffer with weighting) but not same artefact (no time-decay, no per-category half-life, no cracked-bowl noise reservoir).

results.name: Inducing Epistemological Humility LLMs Targeted SFT
results.description: HypoTermInstruct targeted SFT teaches LLM to acknowledge knowledge limits via synthetic hypothetical terms — induces epistemological humility.
results.rationale: Same EPISTEMIC HUMILITY goal as cracked-bowl reservoir but the mechanism is wholly different: targeted SFT on synthetic hypothetical-term data vs. retaining a noise reservoir at full weight in the loss schedule. Keyword overlap >=2 forces a hit; not a strong artefact match.

results.name: Enhancing Hallucination Detection Noise Injection
results.description: Noise injection perturbs intermediate representations + prediction-layer sampling — captures epistemic + aleatoric uncertainty.
results.rationale: Noise-injection at representation/sampling layer for uncertainty is conceptually adjacent to cracked-bowl noise reservoir but the artefact (activation/representation noise during inference) differs from the candidate (training-data sample reservoir kept at full weight). Keyword count forces hit.

results.name: Engineering Consistent Procedures Epistemic Stability LLM
results.description: Engineering procedures for industrial LLM hallucination reduction + epistemic stability.
results.rationale: Generic engineering procedures for epistemic stability/hallucination reduction. Does not describe per-sample timestamped patina decay or cracked-bowl noise-reservoir mechanism. Vague industrial-procedures paper.

results.name: Effect Noise LLM Training Algorithmic CoT
results.description: TInt framework injects dynamic + static noise into algorithmic CoT training data.
results.rationale: TInt dynamic+static noise into training data is functionally close to controlled-imperfection cracked-bowl noise samples in training. Keyword count forces hit; covers controlled-imperfection-in-training mechanism though not timestamped decay.

results.name: Scalable Strategies Continual Learning Replay
results.description: Scalable CL with replay buffers + adaptive scheduling.
results.rationale: Replay-buffer + adaptive scheduling for CL covers the broad replay-buffer family but candidate is narrower (per-sample timestamped exponential half-life). Semantic forced to threshold; functionally adjacent.

decision_rationale: Independent rescoring yields 6/8 hits. Result #1 MSSR (Ebbinghaus time-dependent decay + inverse-retention weighted replay) is a near-exact artefact + effect match for per-sample timestamped patina loss-weight attenuation; result #2 FOREVER (forgetting-curve replay with virtual Ebbinghaus days) is also a near-exact match. Together these two papers cover the core WABI-PATINA mechanism (timestamped per-sample loss-weight decay + replay) at the v5 rubric 0.7 threshold (same artefact + same effect). Additional papers (#4 epistemic humility SFT, #5 noise injection epistemic, #7 TInt training noise, #8 scalable CL replay) cover the cracked-bowl + replay-schedule sub-mechanisms. With 6 hits and 2 strong artefact-twin matches (MSSR and FOREVER), the candidate is not sufficiently novel: FAIL.
